common.moreColumnsOnWiderScreen: Additional details are available on wider screens.

home.pageDescription: Free online tool for mastering memorized deck systems like Mnemonica, Aronson, Memorandum, Redford, Particle, and Elephant.
home.intro: Hope these tools help you level up your memorized deck stack learning. Check out the <guideLink>guide</guideLink> for a walkthrough of everything MemDeck offers. Need a hand or think something's missing? Hit me up on <githubLink>Github</githubLink>!
home.firstTimer: Hey there, first-timer! Pick your favorite memorized deck stack below to unlock all the cool features. You can switch it up anytime using the selector at the bottom of the menu.
home.guideCardDescription: Learn about all the training modes and how to get the most out of MemDeck.
home.whatIsMemdeck: A memorized deck is a full deck of cards in a specific order that you commit to memory — unlocking powerful card magic and mental feats.
home.introNew: MemDeck helps you master your memorized deck through flashcard drills, ACAAN practice, and handy utilities. Pick a stack below to get started — it's free and runs entirely in your browser.
home.introReturning: Ready to train? Jump into a session or explore your tools below.
home.stackPickerHint: Not sure? Mnemonica and Aronson are popular starting points.
home.featureFlashcardDescription: Drill card-to-number and number-to-card recall with timed sessions.
home.featureAcaanDescription: Calculate cut depths for Any Card At Any Number effects.
home.featureToolboxDescription: Stack lookup, faro shuffles, card spelling, and stay-stack sequences.
home.featureStatsDescription: Track your accuracy, streaks, and session history over time.
home.featureSpotCheckDescription: Train spotting anomalies — missing, swapped, or moved cards in your deck.
home.noStatsYet: Complete a session to start tracking your progress.
home.switchStackHint: you can switch stacks using the selector at the bottom of the menu.
home.whyNotAiTitle: Why a dedicated app instead of AI?
home.whyNotAiIntro: ChatGPT and Claude can hallucinate card positions — and training with wrong answers is worse than not training at all.
home.whyNotAiAccuracyDesc: Stack data is hardcoded and verified. AI models can get card positions wrong.
home.whyNotAiSpeedDesc: Tap-based UI instead of typing prompts and waiting for responses.
home.whyNotAiOfflineDesc: Train backstage, on the subway, or on a plane — no internet needed.
home.whyNotAiVisualDesc: Seeing actual card faces engages visual memory differently than reading text.
home.whyNotAiProgressDesc: Accuracy charts, session history, and streaks — AI chats have no memory.

flashcard.pageDescription: Practice your memorized deck with interactive flashcard drills. Train card-to-number, number-to-card, or both.
flashcard.title: Flashcard
flashcard.modeCardOnlyDescription: In this mode, you'll see a card and need to guess its position in the stack from 5 options.
flashcard.modeBothModesDescription: In this mode, you'll be randomly shown either a card or a number, guess its match from 5 options.
flashcard.modeNumberOnlyDescription: In this mode, you'll see a number and need to pick the corresponding card from 5 options.
flashcard.modeNeighborDescription: In this mode, you'll see a card with a direction arrow and need to pick the neighboring card in the stack.

acaan.pageDescription: Practice Any Card At Any Number calculations for your memorized deck stack.
acaan.title: ACAAN

spotCheck.pageDescription: Train your ability to spot anomalies in your memorized deck — missing cards, swapped cards, or moved cards.
spotCheck.title: Spot Check
spotCheck.modeMissingDescription: One card is removed from the deck. Tap next to the gap to spot it.
spotCheck.modeSwappedDescription: Two cards swap positions. Tap either misplaced card.
spotCheck.modeMovedDescription: One card is moved to a wrong position. Find the moved card.

toolbox.pageDescription: Memorized deck utilities for stack analysis, card lookups, and performance preparation.
toolbox.title: Toolbox

lookup.title: Stack Lookup
lookup.description: Search your stack by card name or position number.
lookup.searchAriaLabel: Search cards by name or position

faro.title: Faro Shuffle
faro.description: See the effect of perfect faro shuffles on your stack order.

spelling.title: Card Spelling
spelling.description: See how many letters each card's name has and where spelling lands in your stack.
spelling.searchAriaLabel: Search cards by name or position

sequences.title: Stay Stack Sequences
sequences.description: View the cyclical sequences formed by dealing every Nth card from your stack.

encouragement.improvement: Great improvement! Your accuracy is trending up.
encouragement.keepGoing: Every session builds your memory. Keep at it!

resetButton.label: Reset app
resetButton.modalBody: Choose what to reset. This cannot be undone.
resetButton.resetSettingsDescription: Clears preferences and UI state. Your training history and stats are kept.
resetButton.fullResetDescription: Clears all data including training history, statistics, and preferences.

pwaInstall.iosStep1: Tap the Share button at the bottom of the screen
pwaInstall.iosStep2: Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen"
pwaInstall.androidStep1: Tap the menu (three dots) in the top right
pwaInstall.androidStep2: Tap "Add to Home Screen" or "Install App"

errors.languageLoadFailed: Could not load your preferred language. Using English.

stats.pageDescription: Track your memorized deck training progress with accuracy charts and session history.
stats.title: Stats
stats.noData: No training data yet. Complete a Flashcard, ACAAN, or Spot Check session to see your stats here.
stats.noStackStats: No stats yet. Complete a session to see per-stack data.

resources.pageDescription: Curated books and resources for memorized deck magic, including Mnemonica by Tamariz and The Aronson Approach.
resources.title: Memorized Deck Resources
resources.intro: Explore a curated collection of resources to help you master the art of memorized decks. Missing a resource? Give me a shout or drop a pull request on <githubLink>Github</githubLink>.

descriptions.mnemonica: The definitive work on the Mnemonica stack with tricks, principles, and deep theories.
descriptions.aronsonApproach: Introduces the Aronson Stack, its applications, and several powerful routines.
descriptions.boundToPlease: Compilation of Aronson's early works, essential for stack work.
descriptions.memoriesAreMade: A shorter work that provides practical applications for the memorized deck.
descriptions.magicRainbow: Not just about memorized decks, but essential for deepening magical thinking.
descriptions.inOrderToAmaze: Brilliant routines and effects with memorized deck techniques
descriptions.memorandum: Unique memorized deck with powerful effects and deep insights.
descriptions.temporarilyOutOfOrder: A deep dive into the Redford stack with versatile routines and stack-independent principles.
descriptions.applesauce: Expands on the Redford stack with new effects, ideas, and applications for stack work.
descriptions.sleightlyOutOfOrder: Advanced techniques and effects using a memorized stack with sleight-of-hand integrations.
descriptions.particleSystem: New memorized deck that allows magicians to cut to any named card without looking, featuring built-in benefits and a variety of powerful routines.
descriptions.conjuringArchive: Searchable magic book contents — the Stacked Deck Stuff section catalogues references to memorized deck work.

share.label: Share MemDeck
share.message: I'm training my memorized deck with MemDeck — a free tool for magicians. Try it: https://memdeck.org
share.nudgeMessage: Enjoying MemDeck? Share it with a fellow magician.

about.pageDescription: Contact information and links for the MemDeck project.
about.title: About
about.intro: MemDeck is a side project built by Julien Roussel. Feel free to reach out through any of the links below.
about.githubDescription: Source code, bug reports, and feature requests
about.linktreeDescription: More about me and my other projects

guide.pageDescription: Learn how to use MemDeck's training modes: Flashcard drills, ACAAN, session tracking, and tips for mastering your stack.
guide.title: Guide
guide.subtitle: Everything you need to know about MemDeck and its features.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.intro: MemDeck is a training tool for magicians and memory enthusiasts who want to master a memorized deck. It helps you drill card positions through flashcard exercises and utilities so you can recall any card at any position — instantly.
gettingStarted.selectStack: To get started, select a stack using the dropdown at the bottom of the sidebar. MemDeck supports six memorized deck systems:
gettingStarted.alertDescription: Most training tools require a selected stack to work. Pick one to unlock Flashcard, ACAAN, and Toolbox modes.

flashcardTraining.title: Flashcard Training
flashcardTraining.intro: The main training mode. You're shown a prompt and must recall the matching card or position in your selected stack. Four modes are available:
flashcardTraining.cardOnlyItem: <bold>Card Only</bold> — shown a card, recall its position number
flashcardTraining.numberOnlyItem: <bold>Number Only</bold> — shown a position number, recall the card
flashcardTraining.neighborItem: <bold>Neighbor</bold> — shown a card with a direction arrow, pick the adjacent card in the stack
flashcardTraining.settings: Tap the gear icon to switch between Position and Neighbor modes and adjust timer settings. Use the target icon to start a structured session with 10, 20, 30, or 52 card presets, or practice free-form without a session.

acaanTraining.title: ACAAN Training
acaanTraining.intro: ACAAN stands for "Any Card At Any Number" — a classic effect where a spectator names any card and any number, and the card is found at that exact position in the deck.
acaanTraining.description: In this mode, you're shown a card and a target position. Your job is to calculate the cut depth needed to move the card to that position. Enter a value from 0 to 51. Timer and session options work the same as Flashcard mode.

spotCheckTraining.title: Spot Check Training
spotCheckTraining.intro: Train your ability to quickly detect anomalies in your memorized deck spread. Three modes are available:
spotCheckTraining.missingItem: <bold>Missing Card</bold> — one card is removed; identify which one is absent
spotCheckTraining.swappedItem: <bold>Swapped Cards</bold> — two cards swap positions; tap either misplaced card
spotCheckTraining.movedItem: <bold>Moved Card</bold> — one card moves to a wrong spot; find the moved card
spotCheckTraining.settings: Use the gear icon to switch between modes and adjust timer settings. Start a structured session with the target icon.

toolbox.title: Toolbox
toolbox.description: A collection of memorized deck utilities for stack analysis and performance work. The toolbox includes:
toolbox.lookupItem: <bold>Stack Lookup</bold> — search your stack by card name or position number
toolbox.faroItem: <bold>Faro Shuffle</bold> — see the effect of perfect faro shuffles on your stack order
toolbox.spellingItem: <bold>Card Spelling</bold> — explore how many letters each card's name has and where spelling lands in your stack
toolbox.sequencesItem: <bold>Stay Stack Sequences</bold> — view the cyclical sequences formed by dealing every Nth card

stats.title: Stats
stats.description: Track your progress over time. The Stats page includes overview cards, an accuracy trend chart (filterable by mode and flashcard sub-mode), a per-stack breakdown table, and paginated session history. All data is stored locally in your browser.

resources.title: Resources
resources.description: A curated collection of books and PDFs on memorized deck theory, routines, and practice techniques from leading authors in the field.

tips.title: Tips
tips.dataLocal: All your data stays in your browser — nothing is sent to a server.
tips.darkMode: Toggle between dark and light mode using the icon in the header.
tips.gearIcon: In training modes, tap the gear icon to adjust mode and timer settings, or the target icon to start a session.
tips.switchStacks: Switching stacks doesn't lose your data — progress is tracked per stack.
tips.pwa: Install MemDeck as an app on your device for offline access.
tips.language: Change the display language using the language selector in the navigation menu.
tips.reset: Use the reset button in the menu to clear all data and start fresh.
